title: ocotea andina
intro: Ocotea andina is a species of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae, native to the Andes mountain range in South America. It is often referred to as Andina or 'Mountain Cinnamon' due to its sweet, cinnamon-like fragrance. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ocotea andina include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting immune function, with herbal preparations such as teas and tinctures being commonly used to harness its medicinal properties.
